# Generate the deployment job graph from a small catalog

Delivery workflows keep each Deployment Unit visible as a separate GitHub Actions job, but their static job graph is generated from a single Deployment Catalog because Actions cannot create dynamic `needs` relationships at runtime. The catalog records only delivery-specific dependencies and supported targets while workspace dependencies are inferred from package manifests, commands remain owned by their workspaces, and a dependency-free deterministic generator plus a QA drift check keeps the generated workflow from becoming a parallel maintenance surface. Each selected job builds only the transitive workspace packages derived from its manifest graph; compiled package output is not maintained as a separate Actions cache.
